definitions.text: Having a small width; not wide; having opposite edges or sides that are close, especially by comparison to length or depth.
Of little extent; very limited; circumscribed.
A narrow passage, especially a contracted part of a stream, lake, or sea; a strait connecting two bodies of water.
examples: the narrows of New York harbor

definitions.text: To reduce in width or extent; to contract.
examples: We need to narrow the search.

definitions.text: To partially lower one's eyelids in a way usually taken to suggest a defensive, aggressive or penetrating look.
examples: He stepped in front of me, narrowing his eyes to slits.
She wagged her finger in his face, and her eyes narrowed.
